formatted_address: 835 S Cobble Creek Blvd, Nixa, MO 65714, USA
pitch_rationale: The primary residence displays a complex multi-section hip roof with clearly visible but moderately proportioned ridge lines. Shadow projections beyond the eaves, referenced against the 50 ft scale bar, extend approximately 6–9 feet — consistent with a 6:12 pitch at typical midday sun angles for Missouri. The visible roof planes occupy a balanced portion of the aerial view (neither flat nor dramatically steep), and the ridge-to-eave width ratio shows the ridges are meaningfully shorter than the eave footprint but not dramatically so. The hip returns and intersecting planes show characteristic moderate-slope geometry. Regional context (Nixa, MO suburban construction) strongly supports a 5:12–7:12 range, and the aggregate of shadow length, ridge prominence, and roof plane visibility converges on 6:12 as the most probable dominant pitch.
footprint_rationale: Identified the central gray multi-section hip-roofed home as the subject property at 835 S Cobble Creek. Bounding box established at approximately 280×295 pixels (54.7 ft × 57.7 ft). Applied a 78% fill factor to account for the irregular shape with multiple wing projections and tree obscurement, yielding ~64,400 sq px × 0.038225 = 2,462 sq ft. Ridge lines identified on the main body and 2 wing sections (~175 px total). Hip lines traced at approximately 8 corners across sections (~570 px). Valleys estimated at 4–5 junction points where wings meet the main roof (~200 px). Rake edges minimal as this is predominantly a hip-style roof (~40 px). Eave lines traced around the full lower perimeter including all wing sections (~730 px). Confidence is moderate-low due to partial tree coverage over roof edges and oblique satellite viewing angle making precise tracing difficult.
